title: Heteropappus oharae
taxonomy: Heteropappus oharae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to East Asia and is also known as Oohara dandelion. The classification of Heteropappus oharae is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Asterales, the family Asteraceae, the genus Heteropappus, and the species H. oharae. This plant is closely related to other species in the genus Heteropappus, which is a group of dandelion-like plants. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Heteropappus oharae within the plant kingdom.

properties: Heteropappus oharae is reported to have various medicinal properties, although specific information is limited. constituents: Heteropappus oharae is a plant species, but information about its active constituents is limited. intro: Heteropappus oharae, also known as the Ochrocarp Daisy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to the prevention and treatment of various diseases. The bioactive constituents of Heteropappus oharae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Heteropappus oharae is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, and is believed to aid in the relief of digestive issues and respiratory problems.
